data.title: NFLX: Netflix Stock Pops 3% as Streamer Post Record Net Income, Floats Bold Outlook
data.description:
Key points:
  • Netflix stock jumps in after-hours
  • Net income hits record $2.9 billion
  • Bold guidance despite tariff threats

King of streaming posted a record net income of $2.9 billion thanks to ads and price hikes. But can it survive the next plot twist — tariffs?

🎬 Netflix Shares Rise 3% After Earnings

  • Netflix dropped a showstopper of an earnings report Thursday after the bell — record net income, an EPS beat, and a stock that’s ready to pop 3% at the open on Monday (because today’s markets are closed for Good Friday). Not bad for a week of wild swings for tech stocks, rattled by tariff fears.
  • If you’ve been in markets long enough, you know the question is never just “What happened?” It’s “What’s going to wreck the vibe?” One particular market theme is looming to test how resilient Netflix is. But before that, let’s check the numbers.

💿 Smashing Q1 Like a Streaming Boss

  • Netflix hauled in $10.5 billion in revenue for the first quarter, up 13% year-over-year. Net income surged to a record $2.9 billion — a cool $600 million jump from the same period last year. Earnings per share came in at $6.61, smashing Wall Street’s $5.71 estimate.
  • Investors cheered the headline, but what really caught their eye was Netflix raising full-year revenue guidance to a range between $43.5 billion and $44.5 billion. That’s not just growth — that’s sequel-level confidence.

📺 The Big “What If”: Is Netflix Recession-Proof?

  • Looking for fresh subscriber numbers? Good luck. Netflix officially said “nope” to reporting them quarterly. The final tally from Q4 2024 showed 19 million new accounts — the biggest jump ever — bringing the total user base north of 300 million. Then they ghosted the metric altogether.
  • Of course, no earnings binge is complete without a little economic dread. Donald Trump’s tariffs are threatening to hurt ad revenue if advertisers pull their budget from those paid marketing activities.
  • After a rocky 2022 debut, the ad-supported tier is picking up steam. In February, 43% of new US subscribers chose the ad plan, up from 40% the month before. Execs say ad revenue is on track to nearly double in 2025.

🎯 The Next Chapter

  • Netflix just nailed a tough quarter and is evolving into more than just a subscriber growth story. It’s making money, ads are finally working, and the content machine is still churning.
  • But with tariffs, Trump, and recession fears flickering in the background, even Netflix could catch static. For now though, this one’s still binge-worthy.

data.title: Texas measles cases rise to 597 since late January
data.description: The Texas Department of State Health Services on Friday, reported 597 measles cases in the state since late January, an increase of 36 since the update on Tuesday.

data.title: CNN NEWS CENTRAL INTERVIEW WITH DAKOTA BAGES, FLORIDA STATE UNIVERSITY SOPHOMORE
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

April 18, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

DAKOTA BAGES, FLORIDA STATE UNIVERSITY SOPHOMORE

CNN NEWS CENTRAL INTERVIEW WITH DAKOTA BAGES, FLORIDA STATE

UNIVERSITY SOPHOMORE

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

CNN NEWS CENTRAL INTERVIEW WITH DAKOTA BAGES, FLORIDA STATE

UNIVERSITY SOPHOMORE

APRIL 18, 2025

SPEAKERS:

DAKOTA BAGES, FLORIDA STATE UNIVERSITY SOPHOMORE

SARA SIDNER, CNN ANCHOR

SARA SIDNER, CNN ANCHOR: All right, we want to go back to the tragedy at Florida State University, where police say a 20-year-old student opened fire yesterday, killing two people and leaving five others wounded, and many, many students and faculty terrified.

Joining us now is Dakota Badges, a sophomore at the university.

First of all, can you tell me where you were when - when all of this happened?

DAKOTA BAGES, FLORIDA STATE UNIVERSITY SOPHOMORE: Yes, absolutely.

So, yesterday, at around 10:30, some of my classmates and I went to the library. It's called Strozier. It's basically right in the middle of campus. About a two-minute walk from the student union. We had gone there to conduct some interviews for the club we're affiliated with.

And at about 11:58, you know, we heard these kind of screams from what sounded like a man. We didn't know if it was a cop or, you know, a student. We just kind of heard ruckus, and then we heard a woman scream, kind of like as if she had been toppled over.

And, of course, we're all confused. You know, we can't really see much because it's more, you know, to, like, on our left side. And the way that the study rooms, which we were in, the way that those work is that half the wall is a door, and then the other half is glass. So, what we can see is, after those screams, a bunch of students that were from - that were studying in the middle of the library, actually just started running, kind of, you know, east, towards - towards the other end.

And, obviously, you know, it was terrifying. A lot of the students that were out in the computer area started kind of funneling into our study room. And a lot of people, a lot of people, just, mixed reactions. Some are crying. Some are trying to keep their cool. Others are screaming, you know, you know, get in, get in. You know, push the - push the table on, you know, cover the door. They ended up lifting up, you know, huge desks that are meant to be computer desks, lifting them up and putting them against this glass wall.

And, you know, in that kind of moment, just hearing that screaming from behind you and in front of you, to your left, to your right, you don't know how to compose yourself. You don't know where to hide. There's nowhere really to run. And it was just such, such a scary feeling. We didn't know anything in that moment. You know, obviously, we're not checking our phone for updates. We don't know there's a shooter. We just - we're kind of unsure of what's going on.

SIDNER: Yes.

BAGES: And so -

SIDNER: I am - I - listening -

BAGES: I'm really happy there were people with me.

SIDNER: Yes, listening to you tell the story. I mean, I am so sorry that you had to go through that and that the other students had to go through that. There are now, when you woke up this morning, we all learned there were two people that had been killed, five people that have been injured.

And there's a lot of details coming out from the police. They were pretty forthcoming about what happened and who this was. That it turns out that this was a student at FSU, they say. That his mother was a deputy, or is a deputy. And that he used her retired firearm in this shooting.

What do you make about how this was all handled, both by police, and - and by the school?

BAGES: Well, obviously, you know, in that moment, when you're kind of just hiding and defenseless and they - there are, you know, law enforcement officers there to give you instructions, to tell you how to walk out of the building, to tell you to stay calm, to give you those updates. I mean, obviously, we are very, very grateful for everything law enforcement did yesterday. And, you know, the way that they handled the situation. The way that they were able to come and, you know, save the situation before, unfortunately, maybe more people got hurt. You know, obviously, we're very thankful.

And all of the news coming out about the student, you know, and his affiliation to law enforcement himself is, obviously, all very tragic. I think it says a lot about some of the things that are going on right now with gun safety. You know, a couple of my classmates and I, we actually went to the Capitol, to the finance and tax committee just two days ago, two days ago, to speak out in opposition against Senate Provision Bill 7034, which would provide the longest tax holiday in history, to buying guns later this year.

SIDNER: In Florida, yes.

BAGES: We spoke out in opposition to that.

Yes, in Florida. And - and, you know, of course, two days later, something like this happens, and all I could think was, like, wow, that's - that's just crazy the way things turn out. Such a crazy turn of events.

SIDNER: You feel strongly that there has to be some better and stronger gun policy and gun control in this country. As a student now who has gone through, like so many students tragically, gone through a school shooting.

Dakota Bages, thank you. Thank you so much for coming on and explaining your story at this really hard time. I hope you're going to be OK. I'm glad to see that you're - you seem to be doing all right this - this morning, and that you had friends and people around you when this tragedy happened. Really appreciate you coming on.

END

data.title: The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. (GBX) Shareholders May Have Been Affected by Fraud - Levi & Korsinsky Investigates
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 18, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of The Greenbrier Companies, Inc. ("The Greenbrier Companies, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Greenbrier reported its fiscal second quarter 2025 financial results on April 7, 2025. Among other items, Greenbrier reported non-GAAP earnings per share of $1.69, missing consensus estimates by $0.09, and revenues of $762.1 million, missing consensus estimates by $136.43 million and representing a year-over-year decline of 11.7%. Greenbrier also announced its "decision to close a facility in Romania," citing "market conditions and a comprehensive analysis in Europe[.]"

Following this news, Greenbrier's stock price fell over 11% on April 8, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/the-greenbrier-companies-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=143875&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

data.title: Lost Money on Movado Group, Inc. (MOV)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky Regarding an Ongoing Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 18, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Movado Group, Inc. ("Movado Group, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On April 11, 2025, in a filing with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission, Movado revealed that in late January 2025, the Company became aware of allegations of misconduct within the Dubai branch (the "Dubai Branch") of the Company's Swiss subsidiary, MGI Luxury Group Sárl, related to sales to certain customers in the Middle East, India & Asia Pacific region (the "Affected Region"). Based on a subsequent investigation, the Company determined that "the former managing director of the Dubai Branch, who oversaw the Affected Region, as well as certain employees under his direction, took actions that resulted in an overstatement of sales, premature recognition of sales, and underreporting of credit notes (e.g., sales discounts) owed to customers in the Affected Region. These actions included the use of a third-party warehouse unknown to the Company's management to facilitate the premature recognition of sales, and the falsification of documents to circumvent internal controls. The conduct occurred over a period of approximately five years (beginning with the Company's fiscal year ended January 31, 2021). The Company has terminated the now former managing director of the Dubai Branch." Further, the Company revealed that "its historical consolidated financial statements for the fiscal years ended January 31, 2024, 2023 and 2022, and the interim periods within fiscal years 2025 and 2024 (the "Affected Periods"), require restatement to properly record the extent and timing of sales earned and credits issued during the relevant time period. Additionally, the restated interim periods of fiscal 2025 reflect a reduction in operating expenses as a result of the reversal of certain accruals due to the lower adjusted operating results." Finally, Movado stated that "management identified a material weakness in internal control over financial reporting, wherein the Company's risk assessment process did not properly assess the risks associated with the lack of functional segregation of duties in the Company's Dubai Branch."

Following this news, Movado's stock price fell over 1% on the same day. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/movado-group-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=143876&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

SOURCE: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: 'The Last Dynasty': Olympus on the Nile
data.description:
By James Romm

In the course of more than 3,000 years, Pharaonic Egypt was sometimes ruled by outsiders. Invaders seized the throne at three points during the second and first millennia B.C., and at one point held it for a century before Egyptian forces drove them out. A fourth era of foreign rule, by Greeks and Macedonians, began toward the end of the fourth century B.C., soon after the line of the pharaohs had come to an end. This "Last Dynasty," as the Egyptologist Toby Wilkinson has termed it, held on — despite frequent infighting, enormous overspending and bitter succession battles — until 30 B.C., when Roman forces took control of Egypt and drove its last monarch, Cleopatra, to suicide.

Unlike the 30 or so dynasties that preceded it, the Greco-Macedonian regime has no ordinal number, but is called Ptolemaic after its founder, Ptolemy I, and the Ptolemies who descended from him, right down to Ptolemy XV (also known as Caesarion), the son of Cleopatra by Julius Caesar. That last, unhappy scion of the Egyptian royal house was killed on the order of Octavian (later Augustus) shortly after his mother's death. This brought a three-century line of rule to an end.

Mr. Wilkinson's account of this homonymous clan begins not with Ptolemy I but the Macedonian warrior-king under whom Ptolemy served as a general before claiming power in Egypt. In 332 B.C. Alexander the Great led his army of invasion into Egypt, crossing south through the Sinai as part of his campaign against the imperial Persians. Egyptians had chafed under Persian rule for the past two centuries and had several times rebelled, so they welcomed this change of sovereigns as a liberation. Nine years later, Alexander, now in command of all the lands the Persians had ruled, died without an heir and left his newly won empire leaderless. His generals, known collectively as the Successors, seized different regions they felt they could control; Ptolemy, mindful of the Nile region's natural defenses, opted for Egypt.

Alexandria, the city founded by Alexander at one of the Nile's seven mouths, was at that time a construction site (the Egyptians continued to call it that, rhakotis in their language, long afterward, as Mr. Wilkinson notes). In time the Ptolemies were to make this the glittering gem of the Mediterranean, the site of the famous Library with its research centers and archives and of the Pharos, a magnificent offshore lighthouse. At the heart of the city, a vast mausoleum displayed the mummified body of Alexander, which had been purloined from its minders by Ptolemy's troops and brought to Egypt as the new regime's talisman. Peoples of many nations made their homes there, including Jews conscripted as mercenaries.

Such projects were financed by the vast wealth the Ptolemies harvested from their dominions — not only Egypt and North Africa but, often, the southern Levant (including Jerusalem and what is now Lebanon), a region over which they and a rival Successor clan, the Seleucids of western Asia, fought several wars. Mr. Wilkinson celebrates the Ptolemies' achievements and allows us to gawk at their fantastical public displays. A ritual procession in honor of the god Dionysus, mounted by Ptolemy II in 279 or 278 B.C., featured "twenty-four chariots pulled by elephants, sixty by goats . . . eight by ostriches" and dozens of others by various animal teams, including four by horses — "how conventional!" A "float" celebrating Dionysus, he reports, bore "a 180-foot-long golden penis."

Mr. Wilkinson, who teaches at Cambridge University, looks farther afield in "The Last Dynasty" than the enclave of Alexandria and its royal house. Bringing to bear his knowledge of the papyrus troves that Egyptian sands have yielded, he acquaints us with humbler folk — Greeks, Macedonians and Egyptians — whose lives are known to us through their preserved correspondence. A list of petitions found among a magistrate's records reveals that an Arab barber named Parates was cheated of his fee by a customer named Malichos; an Egyptian farmer had been thrown out of his house; and a woman named Tetosiris, who had brought suit against a Greek, had been visited by a hired thug who had tried to scare her off.

In a longer vignette, we learn that a Greek who was named after Ptolemy, but whom Mr. Wilkinson mercifully calls Ptolemaios (the Hellenic form of the name), forsook his prosperous upbringing for a monastic life in the gloomy Sarapieion, a shrine containing the enormous granite coffins of the revered Apis bulls. Though childless, he became a kind of guardian for twin Egyptian girls abandoned by their mother. His letters, spanning more than two decades, apparently included records of his bad dreams.

Spelling helps us distinguish this troubled ascetic from his namesakes, but, as Mr. Wilkinson takes us back again and again to the royal palace, its like-named residents begin to blur together. "Ptolemy" was the appellation given not only to eldest sons but to their younger brothers, and daughters often got called by the same name as their mother and sisters. At times the proliferation of repurposed names can become bewildering or mildly absurd. In one dynastic conflict of the late second century B.C., we're told, Cleopatra III forced her son, Ptolemy IX, to divorce his wife, Cleopatra IV, and instead marry Cleopatra IV's sister, also named Cleopatra. The jilted Cleopatra IV landed at the Seleucid court and married the ruling dynast, Antiochos IX, "even though he was caught up in a bitter dispute for power with his brother Antiochos VIII."

It takes an expert historian to navigate this nomenclatural jungle and a skilled author to guide readers through it. Mr. Wilkinson is both, and he makes "The Last Dynasty" an informative and compelling, if sometimes challenging, read. All the same, one is grateful that, on the journey from Ptolemy I to Ptolemy XV and his ill-fated mother, Cleopatra (officially Cleopatra VII), he takes detours into the worlds of Zenon the estate manager, Menkhes the village scribe, Diophanes the regional military commander and other colorful figures whose names do not need Roman numerals.

  • Mr. Romm is the author of "Plato and the Tyrant: The Fall of Greece's Greatest Dynasty and the Making of a Philosophic Masterpiece," to be published in May.

data.title: Telnyx Applauds Fifth Circuit Ruling in Favor of AT&T
data.description:
Opinion Affirms Arguments Telnyx Made in its NAL Response to FCC

Austin, TX, April 18, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Telnyx LLC, a leading provider of real-time communications and connectivity solutions, today applauded Thursday’s opinion from the Fifth Circuit vacating the Federal Communications Commission’s (FCC) $57 million fine against AT&T. The decision followed the Supreme Court’s ruling in SEC v. Jarkesy, ruling that the FCC’s in-house enforcement actions violate the Seventh Amendment of the U.S. Constitution. Telnyx also cited Jarkesy in making the same constitutional argument in its response to the Notice of Apparent Liability (NAL) it received from the FCC in February.

Notably, FCC Commissioner Nathan Simington issued a dissent in the Telnyx enforcement proceeding in which he concluded, with great foresight, that the FCC can no longer pursue enforcement actions involving monetary penalties under Jarkesy.

The Fifth Circuit’s ruling is a significant validation of Telnyx’s position. The court’s opinion mirrors multiple arguments Telnyx raised in its NAL response, including the point that the FCC cannot act as “prosecutor, jury, and judge.”

“This is a landmark victory for Telnyx and the entire telecommunications industry,” said Telnyx CEO David Casem. “The court’s opinion confirms what we’ve long argued—that the FCC’s in-house process for imposing fines, like the one proposed against Telnyx, is patently unconstitutional. Based on this decision, the FCC should immediately rescind the unlawful NAL. The failure to timely respond to the Fifth Circuit’s decision will inevitably lead to more litigation in which Telnyx will undoubtedly prevail.”

The AT&T ruling follows the news earlier this week that the FCC recommended reinstating Telnyx to the Industry Traceback Group (ITG) as a supporting partner.

About Telnyx

Telnyx delivers voice, messaging, fax, and more for mission-critical applications, and is recognized for its proprietary global IP network. Through its extensive suite of communications APIs, Telnyx provides secure, reliable, and high-quality services, enabling customers to build world-class communications solutions.

Telnyx Media Relations

press@telnyx.com

data.title: Upcoming Economic Data Won't Account for Volatility Following April 2 TariffsMarket Talk
data.description:
1009 ET - Economic data points coming after Easter weekend will have important caveats, says Deutsche Bank in a research note. New home sales, existing homes sales and durable goods orders are all from March, before the April 2 tariff announcements, and the resultant market volatility. Friday's University of Michigan Consumer Survey will provide more up-to-date readings on the state of the economy, but with a catch. "The interview window for the preliminary April reading closed on the 8th, and those respondents were not able to take into account either the rollback of the country-specific tariffs or the escalation of trade tensions with China," the economists say. "Those new responses could either mitigate or exacerbate the preliminary 6.2 point plunge in sentiment from the March data," the economists add. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0921 ET - A bipartisan group of House members have reintroduced a bill that would end federal marijuana prohibition in states where it has been legalized, and be a breath of fresh air for the beleaguered cannabis industry. Two Republicans and one Democrat are taking another pass at the Strengthening the Tenth Amendment Through Entrusting States Act, or STATES Act, which would empower states, Washington D.C., U.S. territories and federally recognized tribal nations to determine their own individual approaches to cannabis. The STATES 2.0 Act's prospects in the narrowly divided House are hazy, though President Trump said on the campaign trail that he supported rescheduling cannabis' federal drug classification. Shares of AdvisorShares Pure US Cannabis ETF, a popular exchange-traded fund tracking marijuana stocks, closed Thursday up more than 10%. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

data.title: The Association of Polish Fruit Farmers: Fall in love with the taste of Polish apples during Maratona da Europa 2025 in Aveiro!
data.description:
We would like to invite you to the first sampling event of the “Time for Polish Apples” campaign, organized by the Association of Polish Fruit Farmers during Maratona da Europa 2025 in Aveiro, Portugal.

WARSAW, Poland, April 18, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Since January 2025, Poland has been presiding over the Council of the European Union and the apple has become the official symbol of the Polish Presidency. It is a natural choice – Poland is the largest producer of apples in Europe and the fourth largest in the world. Every third apple eaten in Europe comes from Polish orchards! Polish apples are a symbol of quality, health and tradition, but also of modernity and innovation in Polish agriculture, which is constantly evolving.

To mark this special moment, the Association of Polish Fruit Farmers has launched the “Time for Polish Apples” campaign, which aims to promote Polish fruit farming and high-quality fruit on the international stage. The campaign is part of efforts to raise the profile of Poland as Europe's largest apple producer and to promote the fruit worldwide.

Numerous special events are planned to be held in Poland and abroad under the “Time for Polish Apples” campaign, so as to enable consumers to taste Polish apples and experience their unique taste. Event participants will be able to see why they are so highly regarded in more than 60 countries around the world – from India to the US, and even in Egypt. Tasting events will take place in Poland (Warsaw, Łódź, Gdynia and Wrocław) and abroad – including Germany, Portugal and Denmark.

One of these events will take place on April 27th of this year at the Maratona da Europa 2025 in Aveiro. The idea behind this marathon goes perfectly hand in hand with the campaign promoting the Polish presidency of the Council of the European Union, combining sports, health and European cooperation. By visiting an apple-shaped stand, participants will have an opportunity to taste juicy apples packed with flavor and experience their unique taste. There will also be a relaxation area with deckchairs, where marathon participants will be able to rest, and an information area where they will learn more about the campaign, the nutritional value of apples and the scale of Polish fruit farming.

All events are accompanied by an extensive media campaign, including outdoor advertising, media and social media activities. By means of these activities, we want as many people as possible to learn about Polish apples, their nutritional value and quality.

Images accompanying this announcement are available at: 

https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/cdb3915f-4d7c-46cf-91fd-478a8d3b96c7

https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/f8639cf6-bf4e-4e05-9205-a6460f555ca3

https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/6e8dd63e-8ead-4a2f-bd16-6d888fde5305

https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/e63d5dd9-41e9-47a0-be65-73b2eb0bea0d

data.title: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP - MRVI Investor Alert: A Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit Has Been Filed Against Maravai LifeSciences Holdings, Inc. (MRVI)
data.description:
RADNOR, Pa., April 18, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — The law firm of Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP (www.ktmc.com) informs investors that a securities class action lawsuit has been filed in the United States District Court for the Southern District of California against Maravai LifeSciences Holdings, Inc. (“Maravai”) (NASDAQ: MRVI) on behalf of those who purchased or otherwise acquired Maravai securities between August 7, 2024, and February 24, 2025, inclusive (the “Class Period”). The lead plaintiff deadline is May 5, 2025.

CONTACT KESSLER TOPAZ MELTZER & CHECK, LLP:

If you suffered Maravai losses, you may CLICK HERE or copy and paste the following link into your browser: https://www.ktmc.com/new-cases/maravai-lifesciences-holdings-inc?utm_source=PR&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=mrvi&mktm=r

You can also contact attorney Jonathan Naji, Esq. by calling (484) 270-1453 or by email at info@ktmc.com.

DEFENDANTS’ ALLEGED MISCONDUCT:

The complaint alleges that, throughout the Class Period, Defendants made materially false and/or misleading statements, as well as failed to disclose material adverse facts about the company’s business, operations, and prospects. Specifically, Defendants failed to disclose to investors that: (1) Maravai lacked adequate internal controls over financial reporting related to revenue recognition; (2) as a result, Maravai inaccurately recognized revenue on certain transactions during fiscal 2024; (3) Maravai’s goodwill was overstated; and (4) that, as a result of the foregoing, Defendants’ positive statements about the company’s business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis.

THE LEAD PLAINTIFF PROCESS:

Maravai investors may, no later than May 5, 2025, seek to be appointed as a lead plaintiff representative of the class through Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP or other counsel, or may choose to do nothing and remain an absent class member. A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of all class members in directing the litigation. The lead plaintiff is usually the investor or small group of investors who have the largest financial interest and who are also adequate and typical of the proposed class of investors. The lead plaintiff selects counsel to represent the lead plaintiff and the class and these attorneys, if approved by the court, are lead or class counsel. Your ability to share in any recovery is not affected by the decision of whether or not to serve as a lead plaintiff.

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP encourages Maravai investors who have suffered significant losses to contact the firm directly to acquire more information.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE CASE OR GO TO: https://www.ktmc.com/new-cases/maravai-lifesciences-holdings-inc?utm_source=PR&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=mrvi&mktm=r

ABOUT KESSLER TOPAZ MELTZER & CHECK, LLP:

Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP prosecutes class actions in state and federal courts throughout the country and around the world. The firm has developed a global reputation for excellence and has recovered billions of dollars for victims of fraud and other corporate misconduct. All of our work is driven by a common goal: to protect investors, consumers, employees and others from fraud, abuse, misconduct and negligence by businesses and fiduciaries. The complaint in this action was not filed by Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP. For more information about Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP please visit www.ktmc.com.

data.title: Shareholders that lost money on AppLovin Corporation(APP) Urged to Join Class Action - Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Learn More
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 18, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your AppLovin Corporation investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/applovin-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=143870&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1016757&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2flBmSeuem6_I" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against AppLovin Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 10, 2023 and February 25, 2025.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> According to the complaint, defendants provided investors with material information concerning AppLovin's financial growth and stability. Defendants' statements included, among other things, confidence in AppLovin's launch of its AXON 2.0 digital ad platform and using "cutting-edge AI technologies" to more efficiently match advertisements to mobile games, in addition to expanding into web-based marketing and e-commerce. Moreover, defendants publicly reported impressive financial results, outlooks, and guidance to investors, all while using dishonest advertising practices.</p><p>The truth emerged on February 26, 2025, when analyst research reports emerged stating that AppLovin was reverse engineering and exploiting advertising data from Meta Platforms. The reports further alleged AppLovin was utilizing manipulative practices to artificially inflate their own ad click-through and app download rates, such as by having ads click on themselves or utilizing design gimmicks to trigger forced shadow downloads, erroneously inflating installation numbers and, in turn, its profit figures.</p><p>Following this news, the price of AppLovin's stock declined from $377.06 per share on February 25, 2025 to $331.00 per share on February 26, 2025.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in AppLovin stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/9klg" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/applovin-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=143870&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong></p><p>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Initiates an Investigation into Allegations Against Adverum Biotechnologies, Inc. (ADVM) And Encourages Investors to Reach Out
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 18, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Adverum Biotechnologies, Inc. ("Adverum" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Adverum securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/ADVM.

Investigation Details

On March 31, 2025, Adverum disclosed in an SEC filing that "certain of the Company's previously issued financial statements should no longer be relied upon due to non-cash errors identified in the accounting for tenant improvement allowances associated with an operating lease and sublease related to a building in North Carolina (the ‘NC Premises')." Following this news, Adverum announced it would restate its "financial statements as of and for the years ended December 31, 2022 and 2023, included in its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2023 … as well as the unaudited condensed consolidated quarterly financial information for the quarterly periods in the years ended December 31, 2023 and 2024." Following this news, Adverum stock dropped over 12% on April 1, 2025.

What's Next?

If you are aware of any facts relating to this investigation or purchased Adverum securities, you can assist this investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/ADVM. You can also contact Peretz Bronstein or his client relations manager, Nathan Miller, of Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC:

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Announces an Investigation Against Movado Group, Inc. (MOV) and Encourages Stockholders to Learn More About the Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 18, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Movado Group, Inc. ("Movado Group" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Movado Group securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/MOV.

Investigation Details

On April 11, 2025, in a filing with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission, Movado Group revealed that in late January 2025, the Company became aware of allegations of misconduct within the Dubai branch (the "Dubai Branch") of the Company's Swiss subsidiary, MGI Luxury Group Sárl, related to sales to certain customers in the Middle East, India & Asia Pacific region (the "Affected Region"). Based on a subsequent investigation, the Company determined that "the former managing director of the Dubai Branch, who oversaw the Affected Region, as well as certain employees under his direction, took actions that resulted in an overstatement of sales, premature recognition of sales, and underreporting of credit notes (e.g., sales discounts) owed to customers in the Affected Region. These actions included the use of a third-party warehouse unknown to the Company's management to facilitate the premature recognition of sales, and the falsification of documents to circumvent internal controls. The conduct occurred over a period of approximately five years (beginning with the Company's fiscal year ended January 31, 2021). The Company has terminated the now former managing director of the Dubai Branch." Further, the Company revealed that "its historical consolidated financial statements for the fiscal years ended January 31, 2024, 2023 and 2022, and the interim periods within fiscal years 2025 and 2024 (the "Affected Periods"), require restatement to properly record the extent and timing of sales earned and credits issued during the relevant time period. Additionally, the restated interim periods of fiscal 2025 reflect a reduction in operating expenses as a result of the reversal of certain accruals due to the lower adjusted operating results." Finally, Movado stated that "management identified a material weakness in internal control over financial reporting, wherein the Company's risk assessment process did not properly assess the risks associated with the lack of functional segregation of duties in the Company's Dubai Branch." Following this news, Movado Group's stock price fell more than 1% on the same day.

What's Next?

If you are aware of any facts relating to this investigation or purchased Movado Group securities, you can assist this investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/MOV. You can also contact Peretz Bronstein or his client relations manager, Nathan Miller, of Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC:

There is No Cost to You

We represent investors in class actions on a contingency fee basis. That means we will ask the court to reimburse us for out-of-pocket expenses and attorneys' fees, usually a percentage of the total recovery, only if we are successful.

Why Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman

Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is a nationally recognized firm that represents investors in securities fraud class actions and shareholder derivative suits. Our firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors nationwide.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, X, Facebook, or Instagram.

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Announces an Investigation Against TechTarget, Inc. (TTGT) and Encourages Investors to Learn More About the Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 18, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of TechTarget, Inc. ("TechTarget" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased TechTarget securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/TTGT.

Investigation Details

On December 6, 2024, TechTarget disclosed that its previous financial statements "should no longer be relied upon and were being restated" because of certain "errors" that had been identified. These financial statements included those filed with the Company's Registration Statement and related to the "Informa Tech Digital Businesses of Informa PLC." Following this news, TechTarget stock dropped. Then, on March 31, 2025, TechTarget disclosed that it could not file its 2024 Annual Report timely, citing the need to "further evaluate technical accounting matters," related to its combination with the "Informa Tech Digital Businesses." The Company disclosed that "[b]ased on preliminary information," it "expects to record a pre-tax non-cash goodwill impairment charge related to the business in the range of approximately $70m to $110m" for the 2024 fiscal year. Following this news, TechTarget stock dropped again, thereby damaging investors.

What's Next?

If you are aware of any facts relating to this investigation or purchased TechTarget securities, you can assist this investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/TTGT. data.title: Altrua HealthShare Welcomes Heather Nanni as New Director of Ministry
data.description:
AUSTIN, TEXAS / ACCESS Newswire / April 18, 2025 / Altrua HealthShare, a faith-based healthcare sharing ministry dedicated to "Caring for One Another," is excited to announce the appointment of Heather Nanni as its new Director of Ministry. With a profound commitment to faith, community, and holistic wellness, Heather is poised to elevate Altrua's mission of providing affordable, compassionate, and Christ-centered healthcare solutions.

Heather Nanni

Heather Nanni, Director of Ministries

Heather Nanni brings a unique blend of spiritual leadership and professional expertise to Altrua HealthShare. Raised in a faith-filled environment, Heather's lifelong dedication to biblical teachings has fueled her passion for ministry and service. Her extensive experience includes over a decade in the fitness industry as a personal trainer and gym owner, where she seamlessly integrated principles of faith and physical well-being to inspire others. Heather's leadership in church communities has further solidified her ability to foster spiritual growth and build meaningful connections.

In her role as Director of Ministry, Heather will spearhead initiatives to deepen spiritual support for Altrua's members, strengthen the organization's Christ-centered culture, and enhance partnerships with philanthropic and nonprofit organizations. Her vision aligns with Altrua HealthShare's mission to unite communities through faith, compassion, and affordable healthcare solutions.

"We are thrilled to welcome Heather Nanni to the Altrua family," said Randall L. Sluder, CEO of Altrua HealthShare. "Her heart for service, coupled with her expertise in ministry and community engagement, makes her the perfect leader to advance our mission and bring our community closer together."

Heather's appointment underscores Altrua HealthShare's commitment to fostering a supportive, faith-driven environment for its members and partners. She invites the Altrua community to connect with her directly at hnanni@altruaministries.org (mailto:hnanni@altruaministries.org).

Join us in celebrating Heather Nanni's new role and Altrua HealthShare's continued dedication to caring for one another with love, respect, and faith.

About Altrua HealthShare

Altrua HealthShare is a faith-based healthcare sharing ministry that provides affordable, compassionate healthcare solutions rooted in biblical principles. Guided by its mission of "Caring for One Another," Altrua fosters a Christ-centered community dedicated to supporting the physical, emotional, and spiritual well-being of its members. For more information, visit altruaministries.org

Connect with Altrua HealthShare

Instagram: instagram.com/altrua_healthshare

Robert Brown

Director Marketing & Technology

877-972-4871

data.title: Ultimate Toys Joins the 2025 Barrett-Jackson Collector Car Auction in West Palm Beach, FL
data.description:
See Ultimate Toys Up Close at Barrett-Jackson Palm Beach 2025 - Plus a Chance to Win Louis Vuitton

PALM BEACH, FLORIDA / ACCESS Newswire / April 18, 2025 / The highly regarded Barrett-Jackson Collector Car Auction is taking place at the South Florida Fairgrounds in West Palm Beach this month, where four Ultimate Toys Sprinter models will wow audiences with their ultra-luxe and tech-rich designs. Stop by booth WH6 to take a tour of each and even enter a free giveaway for a Louis Vuitton bag!

The event runs from April 24 to April 26, so start planning your visit today. Widely known as "The World's Greatest Collector Car Auctions," it draws tens of thousands of guests, bidders, and consignors annually. A fine array of the most unique and valuable automobiles will be present, and Ultimate Toys is excited to join the event's illustrious ranks.

Ultimate Toys & Barrett-Jackson Palm Beach 2025 Event Details

You will find Ultimate Toys at booth WH6, where we will be showcasing four of our custom Sprinter models and gifting one of you a brand-new Louis Vuitton bag. Please note that you must drop by in person to enter this free giveaway.

After you submit your name, we invite you to check out our lineup. Our Ultimate Coach Sport transitions seamlessly from work to relaxation with convertible sofa beds and two HD TVs, while our Ultimate Limo Sport and its available J-Lounge design radiates classy comfort.

Our Ultimate Cruiser 144 Sport will also make its Barrett-Jackson debut to highlight its double-diamond stitch upholstery, wood-grain accents, full lavatory, and kitchenette. The star of our offerings will be our new Ultimate Weekender. This stunning all-purpose van boasts a built-in driver workstation, an isotherm refrigerator/freezer combination, a 200-watt solar charging system, and more.

Visit Ultimate Toys at booth WH6 from April 24 to April 26 to experience an unmatched level of luxury in the upfit industry; we will also be taking orders! While there, don't forget to enter for a chance to win a new Louis Vuitton bag, the perfect accessory to complement your new Ultimate Toys Sprinter.

Navigate to the Barrett-Jackson Collector Car Auction website now to purchase tickets or get additional information. The South Florida Fairgrounds is located at 9067 Southern Boulevard in West Palm Beach.

About Ultimate Toys

Ultimate Toys is revolutionizing the upfitting industry by transforming top-of-the-line Mercedes-Benz Sprinters into the most indulgent luxury vans on the market. Versatility is a core focus of ours, as we offer numerous configurations that fulfill the wants and needs of families, businesses, adventurers, and more. Our Ultimate Toys models guarantee the reliability of Mercedes-Benz engineering alongside top-tier performance and unparalleled luxury. Contact us for further information.

Vice President of Marketing

data.title: BADGER METER INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Badger Meter Inc., a leading provider of smart water solutions, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing robust financial and operational performance. The company's focus on innovative metering technologies and smart water management solutions has driven significant growth across various metrics.

Financial Highlights

Badger Meter Inc. reported impressive financial results for the latest quarter, reflecting strong growth and operational efficiency:

  • Net Sales: $222.2 million, an increase of 13.2% from the prior year’s $196.3 million, driven by increased mechanical and ultrasonic meter sales, ORION® Cellular endpoint, and BEACON® SaaS revenue.
  • Gross Margin: $95.4 million, representing 42.9% of sales, up from 39.3% in the prior year, benefiting from increased sales volumes and favorable customer sales and product mix.
  • Operating Earnings: $49.4 million, or 22.2% of sales, compared to $36.6 million, or 18.6% of sales, in the comparable prior year quarter, reflecting higher sales and improved gross margin.
  • Net Earnings: $38.4 million, compared to $29.1 million for the same period in 2024, reflecting increased sales and improved operational efficiency.
  • Diluted EPS: $1.30, compared to $0.99 for the same period in 2024, indicating strong earnings growth per share.

Business Highlights

Badger Meter Inc. has also demonstrated strong operational performance, with key developments and strategic initiatives driving growth:

  • Revenue Segments: The utility water product line, which includes smart metering solutions, accounted for approximately 88% of net sales in 2024. This segment saw growth due to increased sales of mechanical and ultrasonic meters, ORION® Cellular endpoints, and BEACON® SaaS revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: The United States remains the largest market for Badger Meter, with significant sales growth in the utility water market. International sales were also noted in regions such as Asia, Canada, Europe, and the Middle East, with varying performance across these areas.
  • Sales Units: There was a notable increase in sales volumes for utility water products, driven by higher demand for smart metering solutions and the integration of SmartCover's sewer monitoring solutions.
  • New Product Launches: The acquisition of SmartCover introduced new sewer line and lift station monitoring solutions to Badger Meter's portfolio, enhancing their utility water product line.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in the adoption of AMI systems and ultrasonic water meters, driven by the need for more frequent and diverse data collection in water management. Badger Meter is well-positioned to capitalize on these trends with its comprehensive suite of smart water solutions.

SEC Filing: BADGER METER INC [ BMI ] - 10-Q - Apr. 18, 2025
